documentation.note: Case study §4 + §99 populated with T+7d kill-criteria evaluation outcome via this PR (chore/ucc-hardening-b12-promote-2026-05-27).

problem_statement: The UCC passkey auth system shipped 2026-05-07 and cut over to UCC_AUTH_MODE=both on 2026-05-16. Production state at 2026-05-19 audit (verified via Upstash SCAN): 1 operator, 1 credential. Four parent-PRD gaps remain unclosed: (G1) no code-level lock against registering a 2nd operator email beyond bootstrap-token gating; (G2) no env-var allowlist of permitted operator emails — the system depends entirely on operational discipline around UCC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_TOKEN secrecy; (G3) no rate limiting or lockout after N failed authentications — parent PRD §7 Q2 deferred to T+7d; (G4) bootstrap-token issuance has no audit trail (only consumption is logged). The T+7d kill-criteria checkpoint at 2026-05-23 will evaluate cutover success; hardening must be live before then so the checkpoint reflects production posture, not cutover-day posture.
proposed_solution: Ship four defense-in-depth controls in a single Enhancement: (G1+G2) Email allowlist gate in /api/auth/register/options keyed off new UCC_ALLOWED_EMAILS env var (fail-closed); (G3) Hybrid per-email (10 fails / 15 min) + per-IP class (20 fails / 30 min) lockout via new src/lib/auth/redis-lockout.ts module wired into authenticate/verify + register/verify; (G4) auth_bootstrap_token_issued audit event emitted by scripts/issue-bootstrap-token.ts at issuance time (existing post-consumption event renamed to auth_bootstrap_token_consumed). Companion operator CLI scripts/clear-lockout.ts for manual recovery. No new infrastructure required — reuses existing Upstash Redis + audit-log writer. Auth-path latency +≤2ms per request.

primary.name: unauthorized_operator_registration_attempts_succeeded
primary.definition: Count of successful registrations of a new operator email NOT in UCC_ALLOWED_EMAILS, measured over rolling 30-day window via Redis ucc:operator:* SCAN delta vs allowlist contents
primary.baseline: 0 at pre-hardening (no attack observed yet, but the surface was unprotected)

secondary.name: lockout_false_positive_rate
secondary.definition: auth_lockout_triggered events where the IP class matches the operator's own session origin, measured over rolling 7-day window via Redis audit log

secondary.name: auth_path_latency_p50_overhead
secondary.definition: Sign-in latency p50 increase vs pre-hardening Vercel function-execution baseline (Vercel logs)
secondary.baseline: Pre-hardening p50 captured from Vercel function logs 2026-05-19 evening

secondary.name: bootstrap_token_issuance_audit_coverage
secondary.definition: Ratio of auth_bootstrap_token_issued events to actual ucc:bootstrap:* keys written, measured per issuance

success_metrics.review_cadence: T+3d (2026-05-22), T+7d (2026-05-23, joint with cutover kill-criteria), T+30d (2026-06-19), annual (2027-05-19)

trigger_1.threshold: ≥ 1 false positive locking the operator (regvash21@gmail.com) out for > 5 minutes within first 7 days
trigger_1.response: Raise EMAIL_LOCK_THRESHOLD from 10 to 15 (or 20) AND extend window TTL inversely; ship as v0.2 patch

trigger_2.response: Profile Redis client; either pipeline checkLockout into a single Redis pipeline call OR revert lockout module + ship without G3

trigger_3.threshold: ≥ 1 allowlist_unset event from production indicating env-var rolled back accidentally
trigger_3.response: Re-add UCC_ALLOWED_EMAILS env var in Vercel + redeploy; audit Vercel env-var change log for who/when

trigger_4.threshold: Operator cannot sign in for > 5 minutes due to any hardening-introduced bug
trigger_4.response: Revert fitme-story merge commit immediately (no env-var bypass exists for lockout); see risk-sub-plan §4.5

C4-C5 (Part 7 break-glass YubiKey before 2026-05-28) — allowlist + lockout must accommodate 2nd-credential ceremony for same operator email

Bulk-advance implementation→testing→review→merge→documentation. fitme-story PR #127 merged 2026-05-20T03:53Z (the code). FT2 PRs #410 (Phase 0/1/2 prep) + #412 (Phase 3 advance) merged. T20 partial-live verify done (S2+S3 live; S4-S6 unit-test-covered). Awaiting T+7d kill-criteria checkpoint 2026-05-27 to advance documentation→complete.

B12 T+7d kill-criteria evaluation: PROMOTE verdict. K1 (lockout false-positive rate) NOT FIRED — 0 lockout events of any kind in 7d window. K2 (latency overhead) instrumentation invalid — audit-log duration_ms measures end-to-end WebAuthn ceremony, not server-side function time the +5ms threshold was sized for; v7.9.1 candidate F-AUTH-LATENCY-SERVER-METRIC queued. K3 (allowlist_unset) NOT FIRED — 0 events. K4 (operator lockout > 5min) NOT FIRED — 4 successful sign-ins across window. Primary metric unauthorized_operator_registration_attempts_succeeded = 0. Operator unaffected throughout window. Full evidence in case study §4 + §99.
